The2024 Paris Olympicsare coming to an end this week, and there is only one person equipped to hand the summer games off to Los Angeles:Tom Cruise.

Varietyreports that Cruise will be making an appearance at the closing ceremony, according to a source close to the Olympics organizing committee. Though the details of his performance are still under wraps, Cruise was spotted filming a scene while riding a motorcycle with a large flag. French blogSortir à Parisreports that the actor was seen driving a motorcycle from the Arc de Triomphe, along the Champs-Élysées Avenue.

DECIDER reached out to Cruise’s representatives for additional comment.

Cruise has already been spotted at several events at the Paris Olympics, including women’s gymnastics and swimming. He also gotcaught in the rainwithHoda Kotb, who is also in Paris for the Olympics.

According toVariety‘s report, the closing ceremony will showcase “new interpretations” and will “take spectators on a journey through time, both past and future” as it highlights milestones of the Olympic Games.

It has also been confirmed that Paris Mayor Anne Hidalgo will be handing the Olympic flag to Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass during the closing ceremony. Could this be the flag Cruise was spotted filming with? Only one way to find out.

How can viewers at home tune into the 2024 Paris Olympics Closing Ceremony? Here’s everything you need to know.

The 2024 Summer Olympics will be coming to an end this Sunday (Aug. 11).

Highlights from the summer games will begin airing at 2 p.m. ET, while the Closing Ceremony will begin at 3 p.m. ET.

The events will air live on NBC and stream live on Peacock and NBCOlympics.com.

NBC will also air the Closing Ceremony again at 7 p.m. ET, followed by the “Best of Paris” highlights and a “Countdown to LA28” at 10 p.m. ET, which will offer a preview of what to expect at the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.

Viewers hoping to stream the event can sign up for a Peacock Premium account, which costs $7.99 a month or $79.99 a year. The premium account includes coverage of the 2024 Paris Olympic Games.

For those looking for an ad-free experience, users can sign up for the Premium Plus account for $13.99 a month.
